key_insight: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings form a stable, multi-phase oxidation barrier on TC4 that follows parabolic oxidation kinetics while retaining and even increasing hardness during long-term 450°C exposure.
engineering_relevance: These coatings offer a promising high-temperature protective layer for titanium alloys, helping suppress oxidation-driven degradation in aerospace and other hot-section applications.
method: Fabricated AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings by detonation spraying (DS).
Aged/oxidized coatings in air at 450°C for up to 250 h.
Tracked oxidation kinetics, phase evolution, and microhardness over time.
evidence_signal: The coating followed parabolic oxidation behavior with kp = 1.83 × 10^-2 mg²·cm^-4·h^-1, while hardness rose from 491 HV0.2 to 665.2 HV0.2 after 250 h.
watch_item: The protective performance depends on phase evolution during long-term heating, so application should consider potential QC decomposition and Al depletion under different service temperatures or atmospheres.
paper_title: High-temperature oxidation kinetics and microstructural evolution of detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings
